prompt: Give me the current operating read for Union Collective. Include whether meeting transcripts are available in the packet.

observations: tools fired: backendDeepAgentProjectStatus, project_lookup, packet_reader, teams_reader, meetings_reader, emails_reader, documents_reader, financials_reader, schedule_reader, rfi_reader, submittal_reader, deepagents_runtime
score.finalText:
Bottom line: Union Collective is in late-stage design and pre-permit coordination, but the job is still being held back by two things: schedule control and financing alignment. The packet does include meeting material, but I do not see clear evidence that full meeting transcripts are packaged cleanly there.

Evidence
- The current packet says the project is “moving through late-stage design and pre-permit coordination,” with “schedule and financing alignment” as the main gating issues.
- Financial/activity evidence is still live: a May 19 email from Eli Hariton says schematic design is targeted in about 3 weeks and design development in 5–6 weeks, but both depend on architecture closing plans and, more importantly, ownership turning feedback fast.
- The May 19 CAD files email confirms updated plans/elevations were sent for the meeting that day, which tells me design coordination is active, not closed.
- The project has a “Design Milestone Schedule” document dated May 9, which supports that the team is tracking milestones, but your source coverage shows schedule/RFI/submittal structured records are missing.
- On the meeting side, the packet shows meeting items are present, including a Civil Design Meeting PDF and a DGI meeting email, but the snippet language is “read-only meetings source row,” not a clean transcript excerpt.

What this means
This is not a clean execution-phase handoff yet. The design team is working, but the job still depends on fast owner feedback and tighter schedule control. That’s the real risk: the work can keep moving in email, but if decisions drag, the whole pre-permit sequence slips.

Recommended next steps
- PM: force a same-week decision loop on open design comments and owner feedback.
- PM: lock the milestone schedule against the current design package and identify anything that is still gating permit submission.
- Finance/PM: reconcile the “financing alignment” issue now; don’t let it sit as a soft dependency.
- If you want, I can turn this into a sharper owner-update version or a risk-only version.

Uncertainty
- Structured schedule, RFI, and submittal records are missing in coverage, so I cannot confirm execution health from the control system.
- The packet appears to include meeting material, but I cannot confirm a full transcript bundle from the evidence provided.

Source coverage note: schedule: missing; rfi: missing; submittal: missing. I did not use unavailable or stale source categories as factual support.

The conversation primarily focuses on their current work situations, educational commitments, and personal experiences related to their professional development.

**Key Topics and Sections**:

sources.title: Re: Union Collective PO
sources.snippet:
Subject: Re: Union Collective PO
Date: 2026-05-19T15:28:40Z
From: Eli Hariton <e@damngoodinteriors.com>
To: Andrew Cannon <acannon@alleatogroup.com>

Thanks Andrew! I'm hoping to finish schematic design in 3 weeks and design development in 5/6 weeks from now. All of this also depends on how quickly architecture can wrap their plans but most importantly how quickly ownership can provide feedback on designs. If we need to wait over a week for feedback this will inevitably slow us down. P
